extra.reasoning:
This is a state machine. The property G(opted_out implies not sent) is VIOLATED via the t_resume_glitch transition.

State vars: opted_out (bool), sent (bool), ticks [0,12]. Init: opted_out=false, sent=false, ticks=0.

The vulnerability: on a send_attempt event when opted_out=true AND ticks>=12, transition t_resume_glitch (line 14) is enabled and sets sent:=true. This conflicts with t_send_blocked (which would set sent:=false), and the glitch sets sent=true while opted_out remains true.

Counterexample (14 events, within the 16-event bound):
1-12. tick x12: ticks climbs 0->12 (capped at 12); each tick also sets sent:=false. After: opted_out=false, sent=false, ticks=12.
13. optout: opted_out:=true, sent:=false. State: opted_out=true, sent=false, ticks=12.
14. send_attempt: guard of t_resume_glitch (opted_out and ticks>=12) = (true and 12>=12) = true, so sent:=true.

Resulting state: opted_out=true AND sent=true. This directly falsifies opted_out implies not sent, so the global property fails on this reachable execution. The state is reachable because ticks can be driven to its cap of 12, optout is always enabled, and the glitch transition then fires on send_attempt.
